response: The 2007 documentary "Zoo" was not more successful than the 1998 documentary "Divine Trash." While "Zoo" was selected as one of the top five American films at the 2007 Cannes Film Festival and premiered at the Sundance Film Festival, "Divine Trash" is a well-known documentary directed by Steve Yeager and is generally recognized as a significant work in the documentary genre. The success of a documentary is subjective and depends on various factors such as critical reception, awards, and public impact, but there is no definitive evidence to suggest that "Zoo" was more successful than "Divine Trash."
